name: llm-application-dev-langchain-agent
description: You are an expert LangChain agent developer specializing in production-grade AI systems using LangChain 0.1+ and LangGraph.
capabilities: Generate production-ready LangChain and LangGraph agent code with async patterns
Design state graphs and multi-agent orchestration with supervisor routing
Implement memory systems (token buffers, summarization, entity tracking, vector memory)
Build RAG pipelines with hybrid search, HyDE, and reranking strategies
Create FastAPI servers with streaming responses and health checks
Setup LangSmith tracing, monitoring, and evaluation frameworks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 39 patterns flagged as potential security risks, all are false positives. The 'external_commands' findings are markdown code fence backticks (lines 27-237) used for Python code examples, not Ruby shell execution. The 'blocker' findings flag 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' but these are legitimate Anthropic model names (claude-sonnet-4-5). The 'network' finding is an HTTP client reference in a code example. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code.
valueStatement: Building production-grade LangChain agents requires expertise in async patterns, state management, memory systems, and deployment infrastructure. This skill provides proven patterns and best practices for creating scalable, observable AI applications with LangChain 0.1+ and LangGraph.
userTitle: Build Production LangChain Agents with Claude
limitations: Focused exclusively on LangChain 0.1+ and LangGraph APIs, does not cover older LangChain versions
Examples use Claude and Voyage AI models, may require adaptation for other providers
Requires Python development environment with LangChain dependencies installed
Production deployment patterns assume cloud infrastructure (not suitable for edge or embedded systems)

useCases.title: Enterprise RAG Application
useCases.description: Build a document问答 system with hybrid search, memory, and observability for enterprise knowledge bases

useCases.targetUser: AI engineer deploying customer support automation
useCases.title: Multi-Agent Customer Service
useCases.description: Create supervised multi-agent system with specialized agents for different customer service tasks

useCases.targetUser: Researcher automating literature review and analysis
useCases.title: Research Assistant Agent
useCases.description: Develop an agent with tool integration for web search, document analysis, and synthesis

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic ReAct Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a LangChain ReAct agent using Claude Sonnet 4.5 that can [task description]. Include async patterns, error handling with tenacity retry, and LangSmith tracing.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner building first agent with simple tool usage

promptTemplates.title: Build RAG Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a RAG pipeline using Voyage AI embeddings and Pinecone vector store. Implement hybrid search with reranking using Cohere. Include conversational memory with token buffering.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate developer adding document retrieval to agent

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a LangGraph multi-agent system with a supervisor that routes between [agent types]. Use Command routing, implement state management, and add checkpointers for conversation history.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced developer building specialized agent teams

promptTemplates.title: Production Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a FastAPI server with streaming endpoints for LangChain agent deployment. Include Prometheus metrics, structured logging with structlog, health checks, and Redis caching with TTL.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer deploying agent to production environment

bestPractices: Always use async patterns (ainvoke, astream) for better performance and concurrency
Implement comprehensive error handling with tenacity retry and exponential backoff
Use LangSmith tracing for all agent executions to debug and optimize performance
antiPatterns: Avoid synchronous invoke calls in production - always use async variants
Never hardcode API keys or secrets - use environment variables with proper validation
Do not skip memory management - unbounded conversation history will cause token overflow

faq.question: Which LLM model should I use with LangChain agents?
faq.answer: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (claude-sonnet-4-5) is recommended for best balance of intelligence and speed. Use Claude Opus 4.5 for complex reasoning tasks requiring maximum capability.

faq.question: What is the difference between LangChain and LangGraph?
faq.answer: LangChain provides chains and tools for building LLM applications. LangGraph is a newer library for building stateful, multi-actor applications with graph-based workflows. Use LangGraph for complex agent orchestration.

faq.question: How do I choose the right memory type for my agent?
faq.answer: Use ConversationTokenBufferMemory for most chat applications. Use ConversationSummaryMemory for long conversations. Use VectorStoreRetrieverMemory when semantic search over history is needed. Combine multiple types for comprehensive context.

faq.question: What embedding model works best with Claude?
faq.answer: Voyage AI voyage-3-large is officially recommended by Anthropic. Use voyage-code-3 for code-specific tasks, voyage-finance-2 for financial documents, or voyage-law-2 for legal content.

faq.question: How do I monitor agent performance in production?
faq.answer: Integrate LangSmith for tracing all agent executions. Add Prometheus metrics for requests, latency, errors. Use structlog for structured logging. Implement health checks for LLM, tools, and external services.

faq.question: Should I use ReAct agents or custom state graphs?
faq.answer: Start with ReAct agents (create_react_agent) for general tool usage. Use custom StateGraph when you need complex multi-step workflows, conditional routing, or multiple agents working together. StateGraph provides more control but requires more code.
